source.definition: to bring something to an end or to stop doing something

explanation.sourceText: The verb "finish" comes from the Old French word "finir", meaning "to end or complete", which itself derives from the Latin "finire" meaning "to limit or end".
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „finish“ provine din vechea limbă franceză „finir“, care înseamnă „a termina sau a încheia“, derivat din latină „finire“ ce înseamnă „a limita sau a încheia“.

source.additional: Regular verb. Commonly used to indicate the completion of an action or process.

target.definition: a aduce la un sfârșit sau a opri

explanation.sourceText: "A termina" derives from the Latin "terminare", meaning "to limit or conclude."
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a termina“ derivă din latină, din „terminare“, care înseamnă „a limita“ sau „a încheia“.

target.additional: Regular verb of the 1st conjugation (-a). Often used to indicate the completion of an activity or process.

explanation.sourceText: A form of ending or completion.
explanation.targetText: O formă de încheiere sau finalizare.

examples.sourceText: The finishing of the project was successful.

explanation.sourceText: Used in the sense of 'bringing to an end'.
explanation.targetText: Utilizat în sensul de a aduce ceva la sfârșit.

examples.sourceText: He will finish the work today.
examples.targetText: El va termina munca în această zi.

examples.sourceText: Are you finishing the work now?
He finished the work every day at 5 o'clock.
examples.targetText: El termina munca în fiecare zi la ora 5.

examples.sourceText: I will finish the chapter tomorrow.

learningTips.text: Remember that "finish" and "a termina" both mean to bring something to an end—think of the English word "terminal" as a place where journeys end.
The Romanian verb "a termina" is regular and easy to conjugate: Eu termin (I finish), Am terminat (I finished), Voi termina (I will finish).
Think of "finish" as completing a race or task, just like "a termina" means to complete or stop something.
The root "termin-" is shared by English words like "terminate" and Romanian "a termina", both related to endings or limits.
